content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() - adding trans_metadata support (note/created/modified params on trans tag)

fields.note: InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() - adding trans_metadata support (note/created/modified params on trans tag)

content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: I'll own ext.py since both features touch parse()/_make_node() - please send SPEC for domain= support and I'll implement both features together

fields.note: I'll own ext.py since both features touch parse()/_make_node() - please send SPEC for domain= support and I'll implement both features together

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Add optional metadata parameters to the trans tag: note, created, modified.

1. In InternationalizationExtension.parse(), inside the existing `while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":` loop, after the token = parser.stream.expect("name") line: if token.value is one of ("note", "created", "modified"), treat it as metadata instead of a regular variable:
   - Require parser.stream.current.type == "assign" (i.e. `name="value"` form). If not present (no assign follows), raise parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value assignment.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError).
   - Consume the assign token, then parse the expression with parser.parse_expression(). If the resulting node is not `isinstance(value, nodes.Const)` with `isinstance(value.value, str)`, raise parser.fail(f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a string literal.", token.lineno, exc=TemplateAssertionError).
   - Store it in a new local dict `metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}` as `metadata[token.value] = value.value`.
   - `continue` the while loop (do NOT fall through to the normal variable-assignment / plural_expr logic below - metadata params must not become part of `variables` and must not affect plural_expr/num_called_num detection).
   - This check must come BEFORE the existing `if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":` branch, and before the `elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):` branch, so it takes priority when token.value is one of the three metadata names. Any other name (not note/created/modified) falls through to existing behavior unchanged - so e.g. a template variable literally named `note` without an assignment (bare `note`) would currently be treated as a metadata parameter per requirements (missing assignment -> error), per the PR spec "Invalid parameter names (not in the three allowed) should be treated as regular variables" - only the three exact names note/created/modified are intercepted; anything else (including with `=`) goes through the normal variable path untouched.
   - Order independence: this must work regardless of where in the comma-separated param list the metadata param appears, interleaved with variables/trimmed/pluralization triggers.

2. Pass `metadata` through to `self._make_node(...)` call (add as new argument, see below).

3. In `_make_node()`, add a new parameter `metadata: t.Dict[str, str]` to the signature (add it to the call in parse() accordingly - order doesn't matter to me, just be consistent). At the very end of _make_node, before `return nodes.Output([node])`, do:
   ```
   output_node = nodes.Output([node])
   if metadata:
       output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
   return output_node
   ```
   (i.e. only set the `trans_metadata` attribute on the Output node when metadata is non-empty; nodes without metadata must NOT have the attribute at all - not even set to `{}` - so `hasattr(node, 'trans_metadata')` is False for those).

Non-functional requirements:
- Must not affect rendering output at all (metadata is purely an AST-level annotation, consumed by external tooling, never referenced by codegen/compiler).
- Must not break babel_extract / extract_from_ast.
- Must work correctly combined with domain= (your feature) in the same tag, any order, e.g. `{% trans domain="foo", note="bar" %}`.
- Must work with variables, trimmed/notrimmed, and pluralization in any order, e.g. `{% trans note="test", user_count, trimmed %}...{% endtrans %}`.

Error message strings (exact, used by tests):
- f"metadata parameter {name!r} must be a string literal."
- f"metadata parameter {name!r} requires a value assignment."

Let me know once implemented / if anything is ambiguous.
